body.title: [triage] Modernize skill-write-path + context-load-violation tests to design-first (stale literal-path assertions)
body.intention: I intend that the skill-write-path and context-load tests assert the design-first canonical reality (design_emit + memory_root resolver + iter_decision_view), so a passing suite reflects the shipped INTENTION->DESIGN->TICKET stack instead of retired hardcoded-path proxies.
body.description:
Surfaced while landing T-migrate-decision-readers-to-designs. Three test clusters assert PRE-design-first contracts and now fail independent of the network-down (Hex offline) failures:

1. tests/test_skill_write_paths_canonical.py::test_sorted_emits_decision_to_canonical_store_not_lab AND ::test_no_writing_skill_misfiles_to_lab_design_docs (sorted portion). These assert /sorted contains 'memory_emit.py' + '--category decisions' + writes decisions/ directly. FALSE since last session's T-design-first-artifact-type rewrote /sorted to emit a DESIGN via design_emit.py (verified: both strings absent at HEAD, count 0). The test's real intent (never lab/, always canonical) is still valid; the literal-path proxy is stale.

2. tests/test_context_load_violation_summary.py (4 tests) call context-load/run's `violation_summary()` -- but `def violation_summary` does not exist in skills/context-load/run at HEAD (count 0). The function was removed/renamed; the tests were never updated. Pre-existing, unrelated to the reader migration.

3. test_no_writing_skill_misfiles_to_lab_design_docs (outcome portion): the reader migration replaced /outcome's hardcoded 'devlab/runtime/memory/decisions' literal with the memory_root() resolver + iter_decision_view, so that literal proxy no longer matches (the sibling test_outcome_updates_the_decision_json_not_an_md_file was already modernized in the migration commit; this combined proof node was left because its sorted half is cluster #1).

**Proposed shape:** update the assertions to check design-first canonical usage -- design_emit.py / memory_emit.py / memory_root / iter_decision_view + the anti-lab/ and anti-.md guards -- instead of hardcoded store-path string literals. For context-load: either restore violation_summary or delete the stale tests (decide which after checking whether the feature was intentionally dropped).

**Scope boundary:** IN -- modernize these test assertions to design-first + resolve the missing violation_summary. OUT -- the ~314 network-dependent inference/bus test failures (Hex/live-services down; environmental, not code).
**Test plan:** proof-on-close -- the three named test files pass against the design-first skills without asserting any retired hardcoded store-path literal; red on the current stale assertions, green after.
**Completion criteria:** test_skill_write_paths_canonical + test_context_load_violation_summary green on a network-isolated run.

Related: T-migrate-decision-readers-to-designs, T-design-first-artifact-type.
